json.title: Healthcare Practice Consultant – AI Training
json.descriptionHtml:
Company: Practice Flow
Location: Los Angeles, CA / Remote (Nationwide)
Role Type: Independent Partnership / Contract (1099)
Compensation: Revenue-share partnership (1099) — no base, no cap. You keep the majority of every engagement you source and deliver, and your share grows as you climb. Projected on-target earnings of $90,000–$150,000 in year one as your book ramps, with substantial upside as your recurring book compounds and you advance. These are model projections, not a guarantee. We walk through the full model on our first call.

Practice Flow is building a nationwide network of independent consultants who bring AI into private healthcare practices — without disrupting patient care.

About Practice Flow

Private practices are drowning in administrative bloat, burnout, and shrinking margins. The tools to fix this — generative AI, copilots, ambient scribes — already exist. Almost no one knows how to put them to work inside real clinical workflows.

Practice Flow is an AI Transformation Consulting firm that starts where the software companies stop. We work only with private healthcare practices, and we deliver hands-on training built for how a practice actually runs. We don't sell software. We sell time, margin, and sanity back to clinicians and their teams.

The Role

We're looking for people who have run the practice — physician owners, practice administrators, clinical directors, and operations leaders — and who now want to help other practices make the leap to AI.

You lead the work in the room: hands-on workshops, team build days, monthly AI clinics, and strategic advisory. Because you've sat in the operator's chair, you speak with clinicians and administrators as a peer, not a vendor — and that standing is what opens doors. Practices come to you because of who you are, not a pitch.

Think of it as running your own advisory practice without building one from scratch. You plug into Practice Flow's brand, methodology, and back office on day one, and you own your client relationships from the first conversation through ongoing delivery.

You don't need to be technical. On heavier AI Turnkey builds, Practice Flow's technical team handles the integration work — you lead the engagement and stay the trusted face in the room. What you bring is the credibility of someone who has actually operated a practice.

What You'll Do

  • Lead the transformation. Facilitate hands-on AI training: half-day workshops, full-day team build days, recurring monthly clinics, and strategic advisory — built for how a real practice runs.
  • Advise as a peer. Guide owners and administrators through decisions you've faced yourself — clinical workflow redesign, revenue cycle management (RCM), interoperability between systems, and what AI can and can't do for their bottom line.
  • Bring practices in. Your reputation and relationships in the healthcare community start the conversations, backed by a curated list of target practices. Consultative, never a cold pitch.
  • Own the relationship. Carry your accounts from first conversation through long-term delivery, surfacing the next step as the practice's needs grow.
  • Sharpen the craft with peers. Trade market intelligence, delivery wins, and referrals with other partners on the firm's regular calls. (Attendance is a resource, never a requirement — you control your schedule.)

Compensation & Partnership Structure

We built this so the people creating the value keep most of it — a high-leverage revenue share that grows as you do:

  • You keep the majority. Every engagement you source and deliver pays you the majority of the revenue, and your share rises as you hit milestones and climb. Practice Flow takes the remainder to run the machine behind you: billing, payment processing, contracts, proven methodology, Errors & Omissions (E&O) insurance, and the target list.
  • Get in early. We're in our founding chapter. The consultants who join now and produce lock in the strongest long-term terms and share in the firm's success.

We share the full compensation model on our introductory call — that's the main thing the call is for.

Qualifications

Required

  • Direct experience running or managing a healthcare practice — as a physician owner (MD/DO), practice administrator, clinical director, or senior operations leader.
  • 8+ years in healthcare, including real time in a leadership or management seat. This is a senior role.
  • An advanced credential is a strong signal: MHA, MBA, MSN/RN leadership, MD/DO, or equivalent operating experience.
  • Deep credibility with clinicians and administrators — you can hold a room as one of them.
  • Comfort with everyday AI tools (ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, EHR-embedded features) and the ability to explain them in plain, human terms.
  • Self-directed and entrepreneurial — ready to own a book of client relationships and grow it.

Preferred

  • Prior consulting, advisory, training, or facilitation experience.
  • An existing network of practice owners, managers, or operators — people who take your call.
  • Working familiarity with HIPAA in clinical settings.
  • Active ties to a focus specialty: dermatology, primary care, dental, physical therapy, mental health, or group practices.

Why This Role Might Not Be For You

We're straight about fit. This isn't for you if:

  • You need a guaranteed base salary before you'll take action.
  • You want to be handed clients and a script — this is a role for people who lead.
  • You'd rather stay behind the scenes than be the trusted face in the room.
  • You want a casual, low-commitment side hustle rather than a practice to build and scale.

How to Apply

We're not collecting résumés — we're looking for partners. Complete our brief introductory questionnaire: https://forms.gle/JYTVmDNfot8cCjW86

It asks about the practices you've run, how you've driven change and workflow improvement inside a team, and why an independent, revenue-share partnership is the right move for you right now.

Strong candidates are invited to a strategic introductory call, where we share current market data, the full compensation structure, and the growth roadmap.

Pay: $90,000.00 - $150,000.00 per year

Application Question(s):

  • Have you completed the required introductory questionnaire at https://forms.gle/JYTVmDNfot8cCjW86 ? (If not, please complete it prior to submitting this application).

Work Location: Remote

json.title: SENIOR DATA SCIENTIST (US Citizen, Fraud Analytics / Criminal Investigations)
json.descriptionHtml:
SENIOR DATA SCIENTIST (US Citizen, Fraud Analytics / Criminal Investigations)
Full-Time | Remote | U.S. Citizenship Required

What a Strong Candidate Looks Like

If you dont have fraud investigation in your background, it's not you. The ideal candidate is a US Citizen capable of passing a federal Public Trust background investigation that has built fraud detection pipelines that fed directly into investigative or prosecutorial workflows — not just flagged anomalies for business teams. Direct, hands-on experience supporting criminal fraud or abuse investigations is the single most important qualifier for this role. Experience in federal law enforcement, inspector general, financial crimes, or regulatory enforcement data environments will stand out significantly. NLP experience applied to unstructured investigative records, case notes, or financial filings is a major differentiator, as is a demonstrated track record of turning fraud model outputs into leads that investigators actually acted on.

Overview: We are seeking an experienced Senior Data Scientist to lead fraud detection and investigative data analytics in support of a federal oversight mission. This role sits at the center of identifying fraud, waste, and abuse through advanced machine learning and direct analytical support to criminal investigations. This is a long-term engagement with a base period of one year and up to four option years. Work is performed remotely with close collaboration with investigation teams.

What You Will Do

You will design, build, and maintain fraud analytic models and advanced AI/ML systems that detect fraud, abuse, and anomalous patterns in complex federal datasets. You will work directly alongside investigators and analysts, providing hands-on data support to active criminal investigations into fraud and abuse — this is not adjacent analytics work, but direct investigative support where your models and findings inform case direction. You will develop regression, classification, and statistical models purpose-built for anomaly and fraud pattern detection, and you will develop and refine NLP solutions that extract investigative leads from unstructured data sources such as case notes, financial filings, and communications records. Day-to-day work includes Python-based data manipulation, SQL querying across relational environments, and operating within a modern cloud platform.

Translate complex analytical findings into clear, compelling narratives for both technical and non-technical audiences, including investigative staff and executive leadership, using visualizations, dashboards, and written summaries that communicate not just what the data shows but why it matters and what action it supports.

Required Qualifications

Every item below is required. Applications that do not meet all qualifications will not be considered.

  • 5 or more years of experience developing fraud analytic rules and models — this must be documented professional experience building fraud detection systems, not adjacent analytics work.
  • 3 or more years of experience providing data analytics support for criminal investigations into fraud or abuse — experience working directly with investigators, prosecutors, or law enforcement data environments is required. This is a core, non-negotiable qualification for this role.
  • 5 or more years of experience developing regression, classification, and statistical models specifically for anomaly detection and fraud pattern recognition.
  • 5 or more years of experience designing, implementing, and maintaining advanced AI/ML predictive models, including both supervised and unsupervised learning methods, applied to fraud and abuse detection use cases.
  • Master's degree, Ph.D., or equivalent in data science, machine learning, computer science, mathematics, statistics, or a closely related field — OR a minimum of 10 years of applied hands-on experience in the same disciplines in lieu of a degree.
  • 2 or more years designing and scaling Natural Language Processing solutions in production environments, ideally applied to unstructured investigative or case-file data.
  • 3 or more years of hands-on data manipulation in Python with Pandas required specifically; NumPy and scikit-learn experience expected.
  • 3 or more years operating in modern cloud environments — Azure, AWS, or GCP; multi-cloud experience preferred.
  • 2 or more years of advanced SQL development with demonstrated experience in both SQL Server and PostgreSQL specifically; both are required.
  • 2 or more years presenting analytical findings to mixed audiences including technical teams, program leadership, and senior non-technical stakeholders, in both written and oral formats.
  • U.S. citizenship required. Must be eligible for and able to obtain a federal Public Trust background investigation. Candidates with an existing Public Trust determination are strongly preferred.

Preferred:

  • Federal Agency Work History

Pay: $60.00 - $70.00 per hour

Work Location: Remote

json.title: AI Developer
json.descriptionHtml:
Description

As an AI-First Developer on the R&D team, you will build AI-powered internal tools and data-driven systems that help teams across the organization make better decisions. You will work with behavioral and operational data from over 30 million websites that use our products - WPForms, MonsterInsights, OptinMonster, Easy Digital Downloads, and more - and turn that data into analytics platforms, reporting automation, workflow intelligence, and AI-powered features that real teams use every day.

This is not a WordPress development role. WordPress familiarity is a bonus for product context, but the core of this role is AI/ML engineering on top of an extraordinary data asset. You will sit between a Full Stack AI Developer and an AI ML Engineer - strong enough on the ML and data side to work intelligently with large-scale operational data, and product-minded enough to build tools that teams actually adopt.

Interested in applying?

Please read/follow the next steps outlined in "How to Apply" at the bottom of this listing.

Attention to detail is one of our core values! This is your chance to stand out :)

To love this role, here's the type of person you are:

  • You're a builder at heart. You don't just use AI tools - you understand how they work under the hood, and you think about how to make them useful for real people, not just technically impressive.
  • You're a self-starter who can take a loosely defined problem, figure out the right approach, and ship a working solution without needing to be told what to do next.
  • You think about the user and the decision first, not the pipeline and the model. When you see data, your first instinct is to ask what question it answers for the person who needs it.
  • You're comfortable owning the full stack of an AI project - from data exploration and model selection to building the API, deploying the service, and making sure it actually works in production.
  • You're an excellent communicator, fluent in both verbal and written English. You can explain complex technical decisions to non-technical stakeholders without dumbing it down.
  • You have the curiosity and desire to learn and grow your skills. The AI landscape moves fast and you keep up because you genuinely enjoy it, not because someone tells you to.
  • You take pride in the quality and craftsmanship of your work - clean code, proper error handling, thoughtful architecture, not just something that works on the happy path.
  • You're a team player who is comfortable working alongside other developers and doesn't take critical feedback personally.
  • You're excited about building internal tools and data systems that may not have a flashy frontend but have massive impact on how an organization operates.

Common responsibilities include (but are not limited to):

  • Building AI-powered internal tools and data-driven systems used across the organization - analytics platforms, reporting automation, workflow intelligence.
  • Developing AI/ML-driven platforms that process and analyze large-scale operational data from 30M+ sites across our product suite.
  • Designing and deploying RAG systems, LLM-powered features, and agentic workflows that generate actionable insights for internal teams.
  • Building data pipelines that ingest, transform, and serve behavioral and operational data at scale.
  • Prototyping and shipping AI solutions that streamline internal processes, reduce manual effort, and improve decision-making.
  • Evaluating and selecting the right models, frameworks, and infrastructure for each use case - balancing performance, cost, and latency.
  • Writing clean, well-tested, production-grade Python code with proper error handling, logging, and observability.
  • Collaborating with product teams and stakeholders to understand what they need, then building tools they actually use.
  • Keeping up with the rapidly evolving AI landscape and bringing new ideas, tools, and approaches to the team.
  • Communicating with the team and supporting your peers using chat, audio, and video.

Requirements
  • 3+ years of professional experience building AI/ML systems in production environments (not just POCs or hackathon projects).
  • Strong proficiency in Python, including production-grade practices - proper packaging, testing, type hints, async where appropriate.
  • Hands-on experience with LLM frameworks and tools: LangChain, LlamaIndex, or similar. You should be able to build a RAG pipeline from scratch.
  • Experience with vector databases (Pinecone, ChromaDB, FAISS, Qdrant, or similar) and embedding models.
  • Solid understanding of ML fundamentals - not just API calls, but how models work, when to fine-tune vs prompt engineer, how to evaluate properly.
  • Experience building and deploying APIs (FastAPI, Flask, or similar) that serve ML models in production.
  • Familiarity with cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and containerization (Docker, Kubernetes).
  • Experience with data processing at scale - SQL, pandas, Spark, or similar.
  • Competent with version control through Git and GitHub. Clean commit history, proper branching, code review through PRs.
  • The ability to iterate and ship ideas quickly without sacrificing code quality.
  • Exceptional troubleshooting skills - when something breaks in production at scale, you can diagnose it.
  • Ability to keep complex systems simple. Simplicity is a core value.
  • Previous remote work experience. You know how to manage your time, communicate proactively, and stay productive without supervision.
  • Personal computer with reliable internet access.
  • Availability to participate in audio/video meetings and be available on Slack between the hours of 9 AM - 1 PM EST daily.

Bonus points if you also have:

  • Experience with model fine-tuning (PEFT, LoRA, QLoRA) and quantization techniques for cost optimization.
  • Experience building multi-agent systems using CrewAI, LangGraph, AutoGen, or similar frameworks.
  • Familiarity with MLOps practices - model versioning, drift monitoring, experiment tracking (MLflow, Weights & Biases, etc).
  • Experience with Databricks, AWS SageMaker, or similar ML platforms.
  • Published research, open-source contributions, or technical writing that demonstrates depth of understanding.
  • Familiarity with the WordPress ecosystem, PHP, or SaaS product development. Understanding the end user (small business owners, bloggers, ecommerce operators) is valuable context.
  • Experience building internal tools and data platforms that non-technical teams rely on daily.
  • Experience with MCP (Model Context Protocol), function calling, structured output, or similar LLM integration patterns.

Benefits


Working for a fast-growing bootstrapped company is a rare opportunity, one we consider a lifestyle choice rather than a job choice. Our positions are challenging, but also come with amazing advantages and fulfillment to those who earn them. Here's what we offer.

  • Competitive Salary.
  • Term Life Insurance and Accidental Death & Dismemberment for all full-time team members during their employment.
  • Health, Dental, and Vision Insurance benefits for full-time U.S. employees.
  • Health Insurance benefits for all employees in Argentina, Brazil, Egypt, India, Indonesia, Jamaica, Kenya, Mexico, Nepal, Nigeria, Pakistan, Philippines, Poland, Romania, Serbia, Spain, and Ukraine.
  • Work from your home. We're spread out all over the world – United States, Canada, Ukraine, India, Pakistan, Singapore, and more.
  • Flexible PTO after 90 days of employment. We encourage employees to take the time they need for a vacation, stay healthy, and spend time with friends and family.
  • Holidays (based on your location)
  • Paid Parental Leave.
  • We happily provide or reimburse software you'll need as well as books or courses that promote continued learning.
  • We cover all costs of company travel (including our annual all-company retreat and mini-team meetups).
  • Additional Perks include AM Welcome Box for new team members, Yearly Anniversary Gifts, and Technology Stipend each work anniversary.
  • We give you the opportunity to solve challenging and meaningful problems that make a difference.
  • Ability to work with some of the best people in the business through frequent, if not daily, interactions.
  • And in case you were wondering: no politics, no b.s., and no jerks.

Location

This is a remote position - our team is spread around the globe! Our home base is in Florida, USA, so company operating hours are 9am - 5pm ET (UTC -5). While full coverage is not a requirement, you must be available during a portion of the day.

Inclusion Statement

At Awesome Motive, we strive to have the broadest possible view of diversity, going beyond visible differences to include the background, experiences, skills, and perspectives that make each person unique. Awesome Motive is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, or any other basis protected by federal, state, or local law.

How to apply?

If all of this sounds interesting, then please submit your application!

Please clearly include the following in your cover letter:

  • A brief walkthrough of your AI/ML journey - where you started, what you've built, and what kind of work excites you most.
  • The most impactful AI system you've built in production. What did it do, who used it, and what made it hard?
  • If you had access to behavioral data from 30 million websites - form submissions, conversion flows, email engagement, plugin usage - what is the first thing you would build and why?
  • Profile links with code samples (GitHub, GitLab, personal repos, published packages, etc).
  • Links to any published research, technical blog posts, or open-source contributions.
  • Other profile links if available (your website, Twitter/X, LinkedIn, etc).
  • Tell us a bit about yourself and why you should be considered. Details about your experience, qualifications, personality, etc are very helpful.

Also note, don't forget to proofread before submitting. Check spelling, capitalization, etc. This is your chance to make your application stand out :)

We won't be able to individually respond to all applications, but if we feel you're a strong match, someone will be in touch shortly.

Thanks and we look forward to hearing from you!

json.title: Junior Fullstack + AI Developer
json.descriptionHtml:
The Role

We’re seeking an enthusiastic and eager-to-learn Junior Fullstack + AI Developer to join our engineering team and help build and maintain features across our compliance automation platform. Success in this role includes contributing to both frontend and backend development while gaining hands-on experience integrating AI-powered capabilities into production features. The ideal candidate has a solid foundation in web development fundamentals, a genuine curiosity about applied AI and machine learning, and is excited to grow their skills in a fast-paced, mentorship-driven environment. We’re looking for someone who is coachable, detail-oriented, and energized by solving real problems alongside experienced engineers.

Qualifications

Required

  • 02 years of professional experience in software development (internships, coops, and bootcamp projects count)
  • Working knowledge of a modern frontend framework such as React, Vue, or Angular
  • Familiarity with backend development in at least one language such as Python, Node.js, or Java
  • Basic understanding of RESTful APIs and how frontend and backend services communicate
  • Exposure to relational or NoSQL databases (e.g., PostgreSQL, MongoDB)
  • Genuine interest in AI/ML concepts, LLMs, or generative AI tools, even without production experience
  • Strong problemsolving skills and eagerness to learn new technologies quickly
  • Good written and verbal communication skills, with comfort asking questions and seeking feedback
  • Comfortable working autonomously in fastpaced, ambiguous, and crossfunctional environments

Desired

  • Coursework, personal projects, or bootcamp work involving AI/ML frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, TensorFlow, Hugging Face)
  • Exposure to AI orchestration tools or frameworks such as LangChain, OpenAI APIs, or vector databases
  • Familiarity with version control (Git) and collaborative development workflows
  • Basic understanding of cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud

Interest in cybersecurity, compliance, or GRC technology

  • Familiarity with containerization concepts (Docker) or CI/CD pipelines
  • A degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience (bootcamp graduates welcome)
  • A growth mindset and desire to be mentored by senior engineers while contributing real value early on

About Cav

Cav enables continuous cyber assurance for High Reliability Organizations that operate in complex and high-risk environments. Our open-architecture agentic AI platform, Compliance OS, automates, validates and monitors cyber controls to deliver seamless, secure compliance as code. Cav Empowers High Reliability Organizations to Achieve 100% Compliance — 100% of the Time.

json.title: AI/LLM Engineer (ChatGPT & Voice AI Agent Development)
json.descriptionHtml:
AI/LLM Engineer (ChatGPT & Voice AI Agent Development)

Location: Remote
Job Type: Full-Time
Experience: 3+ years

About the Role

We're looking for an experienced AI/LLM Engineer to design, build, and deploy production-ready AI agents powered by Large Language Models (LLMs). The ideal candidate has hands-on experience building intelligent conversational agents using ChatGPT/OpenAI APIs, implementing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), integrating external tools, and developing real-time voice AI applications.

This role requires someone who has shipped AI products—not just experimented with prompts.

Responsibilities

  • Design and develop AI agents using OpenAI GPT models and other leading LLMs
  • Build conversational AI systems capable of reasoning, tool use, memory, and workflow automation
  • Develop voice AI agents with real-time speech-to-text and text-to-speech capabilities
  • Integrate AI agents with APIs, CRMs, databases, and third-party services
  • Implement Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) using vector databases
  • Optimize prompts, agent workflows, latency, and model performance
  • Develop multi-agent systems when appropriate
  • Deploy AI applications to cloud environments
  • Monitor, evaluate, and improve AI performance in production
  • Collaborate with product and engineering teams to deliver scalable AI solutions

Required Qualifications

  • 3+ years of software engineering experience
  • 2+ years building applications with Large Language Models
  • Strong experience with:
  • OpenAI API / ChatGPT
  • GPT-4.x and newer models
  • AI agent frameworks (LangGraph, LangChain, OpenAI Agents SDK, CrewAI, or similar)
  • Python
  • REST APIs
  • JSON
  • Git
  • Experience implementing:
  • Function calling / tool calling
  • RAG architectures
  • Vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, Chroma, Qdrant, FAISS, etc.)
  • Prompt engineering
  • Conversation memory
  • Experience deploying AI applications to AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud

Voice AI Experience (Required)

Candidates should have experience building voice agents using technologies such as:

  • OpenAI Realtime API
  • Twilio Voice
  • ElevenLabs
  • Deepgram
  • AssemblyAI
  • LiveKit
  • Vapi
  • Bland AI
  • Retell AI
  • Daily
  • Whisper or comparable speech recognition systems

Experience building phone agents, customer support agents, appointment booking systems, or sales voice assistants is highly preferred.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with MCP (Model Context Protocol)
  • Experience with autonomous AI agents
  • Knowledge of AI evaluation frameworks
  • Docker and Kubernetes
  • FastAPI
  • PostgreSQL
  • Redis
  • Event-driven architectures
  • Experience building SaaS AI products

Nice to Have

  • Experience integrating Salesforce, HubSpot, or other CRMs
  • Experience with Slack, Teams, or Discord bots
  • AI workflow automation
  • Computer vision or multimodal AI experience
  • Fine-tuning or model evaluation experience

What Success Looks Like

Within the first 90 days, you'll:

  • Build production-ready AI agents
  • Launch at least one voice AI solution
  • Improve agent accuracy and response quality
  • Integrate AI with our internal systems
  • Help establish best practices for AI development

To Apply

Please include:

  • Resume
  • GitHub profile
  • LinkedIn profile
  • Portfolio of AI projects
  • Links to production AI agents you've built (if available)
  • Brief description of your most impressive ChatGPT or LLM project
  • Experience with voice AI platforms
  • Your availability
  • Hourly rate or salary expectations

Preferred Candidates

We're especially interested in engineers who have built and deployed real-world AI agents using OpenAI, LangGraph, or similar technologies, and who have experience with production voice AI systems. If you can demonstrate live AI applications you've built, we'd love to hear from you.

Pay: $1,500.00 - $2,000.00 per month

Work Location: Remote

json.title: Implementation Consultant (AI SaaS) US
json.descriptionHtml:
Implementation Consultant (AI SaaS)

Remote | United States

Compensation: In the region of $85,000 to $105,000 USD - based on experience

Help enterprise organisations transform quality assurance with AI.

At evaluagent, we help organizations rethink how quality assurance is done. Our AI-powered platform enables customers to automate manual QA processes, uncover deeper insights and make better decisions faster.

As an Implementation Consultant, you'll partner with customers from discovery through go-live, helping them translate complex QA processes into scalable AI-driven workflows. You'll combine consulting, problem solving and relationship management to ensure every customer is set up for long-term success.

This isn't simply about configuring software. You'll influence how customers adopt AI, shape implementation best practices and work closely with our Product team to help drive the evolution of the platform.

If you enjoy solving complex business challenges, building trusted customer relationships and seeing the direct impact of your work, we'd love to hear from you.

What makes this role different?

This isn't about following a project plan or ticking off implementation tasks.

You'll take ownership of customer implementations from kickoff through successful go-live, guiding customers through every stage of their journey.

Working closely with customer stakeholders, you'll understand existing quality assurance processes, recommend best practice approaches and configure evaluagent to meet their business goals.

You'll facilitate discovery workshops, lead implementation planning, deliver customer training and ensure each customer is confident using the platform before launch.

Along the way you'll build strong relationships, proactively manage project risks and collaborate with Product, Customer Success, Sales and Technical Success, bringing customer insight back into the business and helping shape how we continue to evolve our platform.

As you gain experience, you'll help refine our implementation methodology and share insights that influence future product development.

We're looking for someone who...

  • Has experience working directly with customers in an implementation, onboarding, solutions or consulting role, ideally within a SaaS or technology business.

About the Role:
We are seeking a highly driven Technical Project Manager to lead delivery and execution for a business-critical enterprise platform built on Angular and Java-based monolithic architecture.

This role requires a strong project leader who can effectively bridge business and technology, drive execution rigor, manage client expectations, and ensure predictable delivery outcomes.

The ideal candidate will possess a solid understanding of software engineering concepts, project management, client engagement, and AI-assisted software development practices. While this is not a hands-on engineering role, the individual must be technically conversant enough to engage with architects, developers, and product stakeholders, identify risks, and make informed delivery decisions.
Key Responsibilities

Own end-to-end project planning, execution, governance, and delivery.

Drive sprint execution, release planning, milestone tracking, and project health.

Lead Agile/Scrum ceremonies including Sprint Planning, Daily Standups, Reviews, and Retrospectives.

Manage stakeholder communication, risks, dependencies, and escalations.

Serve as the primary delivery interface for US-based clients and stakeholders.

Motivate teams, improve accountability, and foster a high-performance culture.

Collaborate with architects and engineering leads on delivery planning and technical risk assessment.

Promote adoption of AI-assisted SDLC practices across development, testing, documentation, and reporting.

Ensure adherence to quality standards, SDLC processes, and release governance.

Drive project recovery and stabilization efforts for challenging client engagements.

Required Skills

9–12 years of overall IT experience.

Proven experience as a Technical Project Manager, Delivery Manager, or Engineering Project Manager.

Strong understanding of SDLC and Agile delivery methodologies.

Good understanding of Java-based enterprise applications and Angular/web technologies.

Familiarity with APIs, integrations, cloud environments, CI/CD pipelines, and DevOps practices.

Experience managing distributed engineering teams.

Strong experience running Scrum ceremonies and Agile delivery processes.

Excellent written and verbal communication skills with direct US client interaction experience.

Strong stakeholder management, negotiation, and conflict resolution skills.

Preferred Qualifications:
Prior experience managing Angular and Java-based enterprise applications.

Experience with legacy application modernization initiatives.

Exposure to AI-assisted engineering tools and AI-enabled delivery workflows.

PMP, PMI-ACP, Scrum Master, SAFe, or equivalent certification.

Experience with Jira, Confluence, Azure DevOps, or similar platforms.

Experience recovering troubled projects or managing difficult client situations.

Success Metrics:
On-time sprint and release delivery.

Improved project predictability and delivery confidence.

Increased team productivity and accountability.

Strong client satisfaction and stakeholder trust.

Effective risk management and issue resolution.

Successful adoption of AI-assisted delivery practices.

Reduced delivery escalations and improved execution quality.

json.title: Principal Agentic AI Engineer – Analog Design Automation
json.descriptionHtml:
Principal Agentic AI Engineer – Analog Design Automation

Location: San Jose, CA or fully remote from any US location

Job Summary
We are seeking an experienced AI/ML Technical Leader to drive the development of next-generation AI-powered automation solutions for analog semiconductor design. This role combines deep expertise in analog custom layout, parasitic extraction, physical verification and simulation with modern Large Language Models (LLMs), agentic AI architectures, and software engineering.

The ideal candidate will lead the design and implementation of intelligent AI agents that automate complex analog design and verification workflows, improve engineering productivity, and integrate seamlessly with existing EDA environments. This position requires a unique combination of semiconductor domain expertise, AI/ML knowledge, and software development experience.

Key Responsibilities
  • Design, develop, and deploy agentic AI workflows that automate analog semiconductor design and verification processes.
  • Build AI agents leveraging existing Large Language Models (LLMs) to improve engineering productivity across analog development flows.
  • Develop reusable AI Skills, MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools, and workflow orchestration components with an emphasis on efficient token utilization, context management, and scalability.
  • Collaborate with analog design, layout, and physical verification teams to identify automation opportunities and deliver production-ready AI solutions.
  • Develop robust software using Python and modern DevOps practices, including CI/CD pipelines, workflow automation, and version-controlled development.
  • Integrate AI solutions with EDA environments using Tcl, Python, Rust and other scripting languages.
  • Optimize AI workflows for performance, reliability, security, and cost efficiency.
  • Lead architecture discussions and mentor engineers on AI-driven semiconductor automation technologies.
  • Stay current with advances in Generative AI, LLMs, Agentic AI, and semiconductor design automation.

Required Qualifications
  • Bachelor's or Master's degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, Computer Science, or a related technical discipline.
  • 8+ years of experience in analog semiconductor development workflow automation.
  • 8+ years of experience in physical verification, including signoff verification methodologies.
  • 8+ years of experience developing high-speed analog custom layouts.
  • Proven experience designing and implementing agentic AI workflows using existing Large Language Models (LLMs).
  • Experience developing AI Skills, MCP tools, and agent orchestration frameworks with efficient token utilization strategies.
  • Strong programming skills in Python.
  • Experience with DevOps methodologies, CI/CD pipelines, software engineering best practices, and version control systems.
  • Strong scripting experience using Tcl.
  • Thorough understanding of:
  • Analog floorplanning
  • Device matching and analog layout techniques
  • EM/IR analysis and power planning
  • Parasitic RC extraction and tradeoff analysis
  • High-speed analog design methodologies
  • Simulation methods

Preferred Qualifications
  • Experience integrating AI solutions with commercial EDA tools such as Cadence, Synopsys, or Siemens EDA.
  • Experience with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), vector databases, and AI knowledge management systems.
  • Familiarity with Model Context Protocol (MCP) architecture and AI tool development.
  • Experience deploying AI applications on cloud or hybrid computing platforms.
  • Knowledge of modern LLM frameworks such as LangGraph, LangChain, Semantic Kernel, CrewAI, or AutoGen.
  • Experience building production-grade AI systems with observability, monitoring, evaluation, and governance.

Technical Skills
  • Agentic AI
  • Large Language Models (LLMs)
  • MCP (Model Context Protocol)
  • Python
  • Tcl
  • Rust
  • DevOps and CI/CD
  • Workflow orchestration
  • Analog Custom Layout workflow
  • Analog Design workflow

json.title: Lead SDET-Automation
json.descriptionHtml:
Location: Remote | Type: Contract



About Newpage Solutions


Newpage Solutions is a global digital health innovation company helping people live longer, healthier lives. We partner with life sciences organisations which include, pharmaceutical, biotech and healthcare leaders, to build transformative AI and data driven technologies addressing real-world health challenges.

From strategy and research to UX design and agile development, we deliver and validate impactful solutions using lean, human-centered practices.

We are proud to be a ‘Great Place to Work®’ certified company for the last three consecutive years. We also hold a top Glassdoor rating and are named among the "Top 50 Most Promising Healthcare Solution Providers" by CIOReview. As an organisation, we foster creativity, continuous learning and inclusivity, creating an environment where bold ideas thrive and make a measurable difference in people’s lives.



Your Mission


We are hiring QA Engineers to validate that AI products do what they're meant to do—and don't do what they shouldn't.

AI outputs aren't deterministic; testing them isn't either. You will design golden-question suites, hunt hallucinations, verify evidence citations, and confirm guardrails hold under combinatorial pressure. You will build coverage that survives an LLM swap, a prompt change, or a new retrieval index.

You sit close to the engineers building the systems you test, and you raise the quality bar by asking questions the model itself can't answer. You treat AI as the substrate of the products you validate, and you stay current on how these systems break.

What You’ll Do



Plan And Design

  • Build test plans for AI products across large combinatorial matrices: input variants, user roles, content states, deployment surfaces.
  • Maintain golden-question test suites that catch regressions when models, prompts, retrieval, or content change.
  • Design coverage for the things AI products specifically get wrong: hallucinations, citation drift, prompt injection, context bleed, guardrail bypass, role escalation, stale or unapproved content surfacing where it shouldn't.
  • Translate ambiguous quality requirements into concrete, repeatable test cases—especially where audit trails, access controls, and content provenance matter.

Executive & Design

  • Run structured test passes across web UIs and APIs; investigate failures deeply enough to give engineers a useful starting point, not just a screenshot.
  • Validate evidence citations point to the right sources and that "pending review" or restricted content stays excluded from AI responses where the rules require it.
  • Verify role-based access at the UI and API layers—users see what they should and nothing more.
  • Verify data integrity using read-only SQL queries; cross-check what the UI shows against what the database holds.
  • Exercise APIs directly via Postman, Insomnia, or curl; read OpenAPI specs and form requests without help.
  • File defects that are clear, reproducible, and ranked—structured enough that an engineer can act without a meeting.

Sustain Coverage

  • Maintain regression coverage as products evolve: prompts change, retrieval indexes update, models get swapped, content gets added.
  • Partner with engineers on evaluation rubrics, golden datasets, and acceptance criteria for AI behaviour—then verify the criteria actually hold in production.
  • Stay current on AI/LLM evaluation tooling and apply what's useful: golden datasets, regression rubrics, hallucination scoring, structured eval frameworks.
  • Maintain test documentation that survives team turnover: clear, structured, and findable.

What You Bring

  • 5+ years manual and automation QA experience in product environments, production-grade.
  • Hands-on experience testing AI / LLM-based products: output validation, hallucination detection, edge-case coverage, prompt injection awareness.
  • Strong test-planning skills for large combinatorial test matrices.
  • Hands-on with test management tooling: TestRail, Zephyr Scale, Xray, or equivalent.
  • API testing fluency: Postman or Insomnia; comfortable reading OpenAPI/Swagger specs and exercising endpoints directly.
  • Browser-based debugging skills: Chrome DevTools (Network, Console, Application), cross-browser verification.
  • Bug tracking and workflow tooling: Jira, Linear, or equivalent.
  • SQL basics: read-only queries to verify data state in PostgreSQL or equivalent.
  • Documentation discipline: clear, structured test plans and defect reports in Confluence, Notion, or equivalent.
  • Comfort with enterprise content workflows, audit-trail verification, and role-based access checks.
  • A clear-eyed view of where current AI tooling helps and where it falls short.
  • Curiosity, persistence, and a willingness to dig past the surface symptom into the underlying cause.

Bonus Skills / Experience

  • Understanding of MLOps, model serving, scaling and monitoring workflows (e.g., BentoML, MLflow, Vertex AI, AWS Sagemaker)
  • Exposure to evaluation tools (Ragas, Promptfoo, DeepEval, LangSmith)—read-only or interpretive use is fine.
  • Automation experience with Playwright, Cypress, or equivalent—even if the role is primarily manual.
  • Browser Stack or similar cross-browser/device platforms.
  • GDPR-style data handling verification experience.
  • Pharma, healthcare, or other regulated-industry exposure.
  • Familiarity with EU AI Act or similar AI governance and compliance frameworks.

What We Offer



At Newpage, we’re building a company that works smart and grows with agility, where driven individuals come together to do work that matters. We offer:

  • A people-first culture - Supportive peers, open communication and a strong sense of belonging
  • Smart, purposeful collaboration - Work with talented colleagues to create technologies that solve meaningful business challenges
  • Balance that lasts - We respect your time and support a healthy integration of work and life
  • Room to grow - Opportunities for learning, leadership and career development, shaped around you
  • Meaningful rewards - Competitive compensation that recognises both contribution and potential


Ready to Apply?


Let’s build the future of health together. Apply below or reach out to:
shilpa.shetty@newpage.io

More about Newpage

Newpage is a digital health solutions company. We devote ourselves to advancing the quality of life by enhancing health and optimizing the longevity of people. We do this by, passionately building futuristic technologies for global organizations across the healthcare ecosystem. We partake at every stage from problem definition, strategy & service design, user research, UX design, and agile software development – utilizing lean practices to deliver and validate highly innovative digital health solutions that drive user value and business transformation.

Newpage is recognized by ‘CIO’s Review’ as “Top 50 Promising Healthcare Solution Providers” and Great Place to Work Certified (GPTW) 2023 & 2024.

json.descriptionHtml:
Core team

$160K - $300K/yr compensation

Required Skills

Python
LLMs
AWS
LangChain

About micro1

micro1 is the leading AI data lab for training frontier models and evaluating AI agents. Experts contribute their diverse subject matter knowledge across domains such as finance, healthcare, STEM engineering, and more. micro1 transforms that real-world expertise into high-quality training data, evaluations, and feedback loops that improve how AI systems learn, reason, and perform.

Our platform identifies and vets top talent through an AI recruiter, enabling high-quality expert contributions at scale. We aim to enable 1 billion people to do meaningful work by applying their expertise to AI. As our global expert network grows, micro1 is building the human intelligence layer for frontier AI.

Job Type: Full-time



Location: Remote



Job Summary:

Join our team as a Member of Technical Staff, AI/ML Engineering, and drive the development of cutting-edge AI systems. Work alongside talented engineers to design, implement, and optimize advanced LLM-based solutions, leveraging your expertise in quantitative fields to create impactful, scalable products.



Key Responsibilities:

  • Design, build, and deploy AI/ML solutions with a focus on large language models (LLMs) using Python and AWS infrastructure.
  • Architect scalable APIs and microservices utilizing FastAPI, ensuring robust, high-performance systems.
  • Integrate LangChain and advanced AI frameworks to deliver innovative model interaction capabilities.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to translate complex mathematical, statistical, or physics-based problem statements into actionable engineering solutions.
  • Apply core math, statistics, or physics knowledge to improve model performance, interpretability, and reliability.
  • Maintain high standards of code quality, documentation, and peer reviews within a fast-paced core team.
  • Communicate technical concepts and project progress clearly through both written reports and verbal discussions.


Required Skills and Qualifications:

  • Advanced degree or demonstrable expertise in math, quantitative sciences, statistics, or physics.
  • Expert proficiency in Python for AI/ML engineering.
  • Hands-on experience deploying, fine-tuning, and integrating LLMs.
  • Strong background in AWS services and cloud-native ML workflows.
  • Proven track record using LangChain for chaining LLMs and orchestrating multi-step reasoning.
  • Solid understanding of FastAPI for building scalable APIs.
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills; ability to articulate complex technical topics with clarity.


Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience delivering AI/ML solutions in production environments at scale.
  • Publication record or open-source contributions in AI/ML, applied mathematics, or computational science.
  • Familiarity with advanced prompt engineering or AI safety best practices.


Compensation & Benefits Notice



The national pay range for this full-time position is base salary of $120,000 –$150,000 USD. All employees are eligible for equity compensation, and employees may also receive performance-based bonuses, dependent on role and subject to company policies. micro1 provides a comprehensive benefits package, including up to 100% reimbursement for health-insurance premiums, paid time off, a 401(K) plan with a company match, and additional benefits designed to support a high-performing, remote-first workforce.



micro1 is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, sexual orientation, or gender identity), national origin, age, disability, genetic information, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation during the application process, reach out to support@micro1.ai.



Our hiring process utilizes artificial intelligence tools to assist in candidate screening and assessment. Our AI tools are designed to complement, not replace, human decision-making.



Disclaimer



The information contained in this job posting, including but not limited to role responsibilities, qualifications, compensation, and benefits, is provided for informational purposes only and does not constitute a binding offer of employment. micro1 reserves the right to amend, modify, or withdraw any portion of this posting at its sole discretion and without prior notice. All employment decisions are made in accordance with applicable laws and regulations.

json.title: Healthcare Practice Consultant – AI Training
json.descriptionHtml:
Company: Practice Flow
Location: Los Angeles, CA / Remote (Nationwide)
Role Type: Independent Partnership / Contract (1099)
Compensation: Revenue-share partnership (1099) — no base, no cap. You keep the majority of every engagement you source and deliver, and your share grows as you climb. Projected on-target earnings of $90,000–$150,000 in year one as your book ramps, with substantial upside as your recurring book compounds and you advance. These are model projections, not a guarantee. We walk through the full model on our first call.

Practice Flow is building a nationwide network of independent consultants who bring AI into private healthcare practices — without disrupting patient care.

About Practice Flow

Private practices are drowning in administrative bloat, burnout, and shrinking margins. The tools to fix this — generative AI, copilots, ambient scribes — already exist. Almost no one knows how to put them to work inside real clinical workflows.

Practice Flow is an AI Transformation Consulting firm that starts where the software companies stop. We work only with private healthcare practices, and we deliver hands-on training built for how a practice actually runs. We don't sell software. We sell time, margin, and sanity back to clinicians and their teams.

The Role

We're looking for people who have run the practice — physician owners, practice administrators, clinical directors, and operations leaders — and who now want to help other practices make the leap to AI.

You lead the work in the room: hands-on workshops, team build days, monthly AI clinics, and strategic advisory. Because you've sat in the operator's chair, you speak with clinicians and administrators as a peer, not a vendor — and that standing is what opens doors. Practices come to you because of who you are, not a pitch.

Think of it as running your own advisory practice without building one from scratch. You plug into Practice Flow's brand, methodology, and back office on day one, and you own your client relationships from the first conversation through ongoing delivery.

You don't need to be technical. On heavier AI Turnkey builds, Practice Flow's technical team handles the integration work — you lead the engagement and stay the trusted face in the room. What you bring is the credibility of someone who has actually operated a practice.

What You'll Do

  • Lead the transformation. Facilitate hands-on AI training: half-day workshops, full-day team build days, recurring monthly clinics, and strategic advisory — built for how a real practice runs.
  • Advise as a peer. Guide owners and administrators through decisions you've faced yourself — clinical workflow redesign, revenue cycle management (RCM), interoperability between systems, and what AI can and can't do for their bottom line.
  • Bring practices in. Your reputation and relationships in the healthcare community start the conversations, backed by a curated list of target practices. Consultative, never a cold pitch.
  • Own the relationship. Carry your accounts from first conversation through long-term delivery, surfacing the next step as the practice's needs grow.
  • Sharpen the craft with peers. Trade market intelligence, delivery wins, and referrals with other partners on the firm's regular calls. (Attendance is a resource, never a requirement — you control your schedule.)

Compensation & Partnership Structure

We built this so the people creating the value keep most of it — a high-leverage revenue share that grows as you do:

  • You keep the majority. Every engagement you source and deliver pays you the majority of the revenue, and your share rises as you hit milestones and climb. Practice Flow takes the remainder to run the machine behind you: billing, payment processing, contracts, proven methodology, Errors & Omissions (E&O) insurance, and the target list.
  • Get in early. We're in our founding chapter. The consultants who join now and produce lock in the strongest long-term terms and share in the firm's success.

We share the full compensation model on our introductory call — that's the main thing the call is for.

Qualifications

Required

  • Direct experience running or managing a healthcare practice — as a physician owner (MD/DO), practice administrator, clinical director, or senior operations leader.
  • 8+ years in healthcare, including real time in a leadership or management seat. This is a senior role.
  • An advanced credential is a strong signal: MHA, MBA, MSN/RN leadership, MD/DO, or equivalent operating experience.
  • Deep credibility with clinicians and administrators — you can hold a room as one of them.
  • Comfort with everyday AI tools (ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, EHR-embedded features) and the ability to explain them in plain, human terms.
  • Self-directed and entrepreneurial — ready to own a book of client relationships and grow it.

Preferred

  • Prior consulting, advisory, training, or facilitation experience.
  • An existing network of practice owners, managers, or operators — people who take your call.
  • Working familiarity with HIPAA in clinical settings.
  • Active ties to a focus specialty: dermatology, primary care, dental, physical therapy, mental health, or group practices.

Why This Role Might Not Be For You

We're straight about fit. This isn't for you if:

  • You need a guaranteed base salary before you'll take action.
  • You want to be handed clients and a script — this is a role for people who lead.
  • You'd rather stay behind the scenes than be the trusted face in the room.
  • You want a casual, low-commitment side hustle rather than a practice to build and scale.

How to Apply

We're not collecting résumés — we're looking for partners. Complete our brief introductory questionnaire: https://forms.gle/JYTVmDNfot8cCjW86

It asks about the practices you've run, how you've driven change and workflow improvement inside a team, and why an independent, revenue-share partnership is the right move for you right now.

Strong candidates are invited to a strategic introductory call, where we share current market data, the full compensation structure, and the growth roadmap.

Pay: $90,000.00 - $150,000.00 per year

Application Question(s):

  • Have you completed the required introductory questionnaire at https://forms.gle/JYTVmDNfot8cCjW86 ? (If not, please complete it prior to submitting this application).

Work Location: Remote

json.title: SENIOR DATA SCIENTIST (US Citizen, Fraud Analytics / Criminal Investigations)
json.descriptionHtml:
SENIOR DATA SCIENTIST (US Citizen, Fraud Analytics / Criminal Investigations)
Full-Time | Remote | U.S. Citizenship Required

What a Strong Candidate Looks Like

If you dont have fraud investigation in your background, it's not you. The ideal candidate is a US Citizen capable of passing a federal Public Trust background investigation that has built fraud detection pipelines that fed directly into investigative or prosecutorial workflows — not just flagged anomalies for business teams. Direct, hands-on experience supporting criminal fraud or abuse investigations is the single most important qualifier for this role. Experience in federal law enforcement, inspector general, financial crimes, or regulatory enforcement data environments will stand out significantly. NLP experience applied to unstructured investigative records, case notes, or financial filings is a major differentiator, as is a demonstrated track record of turning fraud model outputs into leads that investigators actually acted on.

Overview: We are seeking an experienced Senior Data Scientist to lead fraud detection and investigative data analytics in support of a federal oversight mission. This role sits at the center of identifying fraud, waste, and abuse through advanced machine learning and direct analytical support to criminal investigations. This is a long-term engagement with a base period of one year and up to four option years. Work is performed remotely with close collaboration with investigation teams.

What You Will Do

You will design, build, and maintain fraud analytic models and advanced AI/ML systems that detect fraud, abuse, and anomalous patterns in complex federal datasets. You will work directly alongside investigators and analysts, providing hands-on data support to active criminal investigations into fraud and abuse — this is not adjacent analytics work, but direct investigative support where your models and findings inform case direction. You will develop regression, classification, and statistical models purpose-built for anomaly and fraud pattern detection, and you will develop and refine NLP solutions that extract investigative leads from unstructured data sources such as case notes, financial filings, and communications records. Day-to-day work includes Python-based data manipulation, SQL querying across relational environments, and operating within a modern cloud platform.

Translate complex analytical findings into clear, compelling narratives for both technical and non-technical audiences, including investigative staff and executive leadership, using visualizations, dashboards, and written summaries that communicate not just what the data shows but why it matters and what action it supports.

Required Qualifications

Every item below is required. Applications that do not meet all qualifications will not be considered.

  • 5 or more years of experience developing fraud analytic rules and models — this must be documented professional experience building fraud detection systems, not adjacent analytics work.
  • 3 or more years of experience providing data analytics support for criminal investigations into fraud or abuse — experience working directly with investigators, prosecutors, or law enforcement data environments is required. This is a core, non-negotiable qualification for this role.
  • 5 or more years of experience developing regression, classification, and statistical models specifically for anomaly detection and fraud pattern recognition.
  • 5 or more years of experience designing, implementing, and maintaining advanced AI/ML predictive models, including both supervised and unsupervised learning methods, applied to fraud and abuse detection use cases.
  • Master's degree, Ph.D., or equivalent in data science, machine learning, computer science, mathematics, statistics, or a closely related field — OR a minimum of 10 years of applied hands-on experience in the same disciplines in lieu of a degree.
  • 2 or more years designing and scaling Natural Language Processing solutions in production environments, ideally applied to unstructured investigative or case-file data.
  • 3 or more years of hands-on data manipulation in Python with Pandas required specifically; NumPy and scikit-learn experience expected.
  • 3 or more years operating in modern cloud environments — Azure, AWS, or GCP; multi-cloud experience preferred.
  • 2 or more years of advanced SQL development with demonstrated experience in both SQL Server and PostgreSQL specifically; both are required.
  • 2 or more years presenting analytical findings to mixed audiences including technical teams, program leadership, and senior non-technical stakeholders, in both written and oral formats.
  • U.S. citizenship required. Must be eligible for and able to obtain a federal Public Trust background investigation. Candidates with an existing Public Trust determination are strongly preferred.

Preferred:

  • Federal Agency Work History

Pay: $60.00 - $70.00 per hour

Work Location: Remote

json.title: AI Developer
json.descriptionHtml:
Description

As an AI-First Developer on the R&D team, you will build AI-powered internal tools and data-driven systems that help teams across the organization make better decisions. You will work with behavioral and operational data from over 30 million websites that use our products - WPForms, MonsterInsights, OptinMonster, Easy Digital Downloads, and more - and turn that data into analytics platforms, reporting automation, workflow intelligence, and AI-powered features that real teams use every day.

This is not a WordPress development role. WordPress familiarity is a bonus for product context, but the core of this role is AI/ML engineering on top of an extraordinary data asset. You will sit between a Full Stack AI Developer and an AI ML Engineer - strong enough on the ML and data side to work intelligently with large-scale operational data, and product-minded enough to build tools that teams actually adopt.

Interested in applying?

Please read/follow the next steps outlined in "How to Apply" at the bottom of this listing.

Attention to detail is one of our core values! This is your chance to stand out :)

To love this role, here's the type of person you are:

  • You're a builder at heart. You don't just use AI tools - you understand how they work under the hood, and you think about how to make them useful for real people, not just technically impressive.
  • You're a self-starter who can take a loosely defined problem, figure out the right approach, and ship a working solution without needing to be told what to do next.
  • You think about the user and the decision first, not the pipeline and the model. When you see data, your first instinct is to ask what question it answers for the person who needs it.
  • You're comfortable owning the full stack of an AI project - from data exploration and model selection to building the API, deploying the service, and making sure it actually works in production.
  • You're an excellent communicator, fluent in both verbal and written English. You can explain complex technical decisions to non-technical stakeholders without dumbing it down.
  • You have the curiosity and desire to learn and grow your skills. The AI landscape moves fast and you keep up because you genuinely enjoy it, not because someone tells you to.
  • You take pride in the quality and craftsmanship of your work - clean code, proper error handling, thoughtful architecture, not just something that works on the happy path.
  • You're a team player who is comfortable working alongside other developers and doesn't take critical feedback personally.
  • You're excited about building internal tools and data systems that may not have a flashy frontend but have massive impact on how an organization operates.

Common responsibilities include (but are not limited to):

  • Building AI-powered internal tools and data-driven systems used across the organization - analytics platforms, reporting automation, workflow intelligence.
  • Developing AI/ML-driven platforms that process and analyze large-scale operational data from 30M+ sites across our product suite.
  • Designing and deploying RAG systems, LLM-powered features, and agentic workflows that generate actionable insights for internal teams.
  • Building data pipelines that ingest, transform, and serve behavioral and operational data at scale.
  • Prototyping and shipping AI solutions that streamline internal processes, reduce manual effort, and improve decision-making.
  • Evaluating and selecting the right models, frameworks, and infrastructure for each use case - balancing performance, cost, and latency.
  • Writing clean, well-tested, production-grade Python code with proper error handling, logging, and observability.
  • Collaborating with product teams and stakeholders to understand what they need, then building tools they actually use.
  • Keeping up with the rapidly evolving AI landscape and bringing new ideas, tools, and approaches to the team.
  • Communicating with the team and supporting your peers using chat, audio, and video.

Requirements
  • 3+ years of professional experience building AI/ML systems in production environments (not just POCs or hackathon projects).
  • Strong proficiency in Python, including production-grade practices - proper packaging, testing, type hints, async where appropriate.
  • Hands-on experience with LLM frameworks and tools: LangChain, LlamaIndex, or similar. You should be able to build a RAG pipeline from scratch.
  • Experience with vector databases (Pinecone, ChromaDB, FAISS, Qdrant, or similar) and embedding models.

Working for a fast-growing bootstrapped company is a rare opportunity, one we consider a lifestyle choice rather than a job choice. Our positions are challenging, but also come with amazing advantages and fulfillment to those who earn them. Here's what we offer.

  • Holidays (based on your location)
  • Paid Parental Leave.
  • We happily provide or reimburse software you'll need as well as books or courses that promote continued learning.

  • And in case you were wondering: no politics, no b.s., and no jerks.

Location

This is a remote position - our team is spread around the globe! Our home base is in Florida, USA, so company operating hours are 9am - 5pm ET (UTC -5). While full coverage is not a requirement, you must be available during a portion of the day.

Inclusion Statement

At Awesome Motive, we strive to have the broadest possible view of diversity, going beyond visible differences to include the background, experiences, skills, and perspectives that make each person unique. Awesome Motive is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, or any other basis protected by federal, state, or local law.

How to apply?

If all of this sounds interesting, then please submit your application!

  • The most impactful AI system you've built in production. What did it do, who used it, and what made it hard?
  • If you had access to behavioral data from 30 million websites - form submissions, conversion flows, email engagement, plugin usage - what is the first thing you would build and why?
  • Profile links with code samples (GitHub, GitLab, personal repos, published packages, etc).
  • Links to any published research, technical blog posts, or open-source contributions.
  • Other profile links if available (your website, Twitter/X, LinkedIn, etc).
  • Tell us a bit about yourself and why you should be considered. Details about your experience, qualifications, personality, etc are very helpful.

Also note, don't forget to proofread before submitting. Check spelling, capitalization, etc. This is your chance to make your application stand out :)

We won't be able to individually respond to all applications, but if we feel you're a strong match, someone will be in touch shortly.

Thanks and we look forward to hearing from you!
